reasoning: Bacillus cereus infection is primarily a mild, self-limiting foodborne illness. Medical literature and searches confirm there is no approved vaccine for human B. cereus infection, nor are there any late-stage clinical trials. A few experimental studies test anthrax vaccines on animals for rare "anthrax-like" B. cereus strains, but a dedicated B. cereus vaccine is not in the pipeline. Developing, testing, and approving a new vaccine usually takes many years, and once approved, it takes time for Wikipedia editors to update the "List of infectious diseases" page. Because the forecast horizon spans only 6 months, it is practically impossible for a newly developed vaccine to emerge and be added to Wikipedia before January 2026. Therefore, I place the probabilities at near absolute floor (0.02) for the earliest dates, with minor increases up to 0.04 towards January 2026 to reflect the mandated increase of uncertainty with time.
